titleStmt.title: Letter from Andrew Philip Magill to William Howell Davies, 11 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew Philip Magill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Andrew Philip Magill (1871–1941) an Irish civil servant to William Howell Davis (1851-1932), a British politician. Andrew Philip Magill acted as private secretary to Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) the chief secretary for Ireland. Magill assumed the role in 1913 and continued after the resignation of Birrell in 1916. Davis held a seat in Westminster for the South Bristol district. The letter is written in the context of the death of the chief crown solicitor, Malachy Kelly (1850-1916). The letter acknowledges a previous letter of recommendation sent by Davis on behalf of William Horner (b. 1878), a solicitor in London-Derry. It goes on to state that Horner's application will be fully considered.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/6463/1

correspAction.placeName: Down House, Stoke Bishop, Bristol, England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Copy 11th April, 1916. Dear Sir William Howell Davies, I am desired by Mr. Birrell to acknowledge the receipt
of your letter of 10th instant, and enclosure, recommend-
ing Mr. Horner for appointment as Chief Crown Solicitor for
Ireland, and to say that Mr. Birrell has taken a note of
your recommendation and will see that Mr. Horner's claims
are fully considered. Yours faithfully,
(Sd) APM
Under Secy.
A.P.M.
11/4/16
Sir William Howell Davies, M.P., Down House,
Stoke Bishop,
BRISTOL.
